insights.recommendation: Reallocate budget to increase investment in Referral and Recruiter Outreach while reducing LinkedIn spending.

evidence.key_insight: Referral has the highest offer rate at 41.2%, and LinkedIn underperforms significantly with only a 12.5% offer rate.

alternative.trade_off: More detailed insights may provide deeper analysis but could increase time and cost.

[{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Sourcing channel performance summary (200 candidates). Referral leads all channels with a 41.2% offer rate and 57.1% acceptance rate — making it the highest ROI channel. Recruiter Outreach is second at 37.5% offer rate with 55.6% acceptance. Hackathon has a strong 32.3% offer rate but only 40.0% acceptance, suggesting strong initial fit but weak closing. LinkedIn has the worst offer rate at 12.5% with 50% acceptance — high screening cost, low yield.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"sourcing_quality\",\"category\":\"channel_performance\"},\"id\":\"src-001\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Offer acceptance rates by sourcing channel. Company Website converts best once an offer is made: 85.7% acceptance rate (6 of 7 offers). Glassdoor is second at 77.8% (7 of 9). AngelList and LinkedIn both sit at 50.0%. Hackathon is the weakest closer at 40.0% — candidates from events may be passively exploring. Benchmark: industry average offer acceptance is 70-75%; channels below 55% warrant re-evaluation.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"sourcing_quality\",\"category\":\"channel_acceptance\"},\"id\":\"src-002\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Sourcing channel optimization recommendations based on hiring data. Priority actions: (1) Double down on Referral — highest offer rate, lowest dropout; (2) Invest in Recruiter Outreach for senior/staff roles — 37.5% offer rate; (3) Reduce LinkedIn budget — 12.5% offer rate does not justify cost; (4) Glassdoor has solid acceptance rate (77.8%) — maintain current spend; (5) Hackathon sourcing works for initial pipeline but needs better closing strategy. Overall pipeline conversion from application to offer: 28.5% (57 offers from 200 candidates).\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"sourcing_quality\",\"category\":\"channel_optimization\"},\"id\":\"src-006\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Industry sourcing benchmarks for engineering and technical roles. Best-in-class offer rates by channel: Referral 40-55%, Recruiter Outreach 30-45%, Job Boards (Indeed/Glassdoor) 20-35%, LinkedIn 15-25%, Hackathons 25-35%. Typical offer acceptance rate benchmark: 72-78% for senior roles, 65-70% for mid-level. Time-to-source benchmark: Referral 8-12 days, LinkedIn 18-25 days, Job Boards 14-20 days. Cost-per-hire benchmark: Referral lowest ($1,500-3,000), Agency/Recruiter highest ($15,000-25,000).\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"sourcing_quality\",\"category\":\"industry_benchmark\"},\"id\":\"src-004\"}"}]

insights.recommendation: Address role cancellation and compensation mismatch issues to reduce process failures accounting for 26.3% of rejections.

evidence.key_insight: Role cancellations and compensation mismatches together account for 26.3% of rejections, signaling process failures rather than candidate failures.

alternative.trade_off: Lower analysis depth and potential oversights in process-related insights.

insights.rag_context: [{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Rejection reason breakdown (114 rejections from 200 candidates, 57% rejection rate). Top reasons: Role cancelled (15, 13.2%), Compensation mismatch (15, 13.2%), Underqualified (13, 11.4%), Communication issues (12, 10.5%), Technical skills (11, 9.6%), Background check failed (11, 9.6%), Culture fit (10, 8.8%), Overqualified (10, 8.8%), No show (9, 7.9%), Better candidate selected (8, 7.0%). Role cancellations and compensation mismatches together account for 26.3% — these are process failures, not candidate failures.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"rejection_reasons_overall\"},\"id\":\"rej-001\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Rejection root cause categorisation. Controllable rejections (team can fix): Role cancelled (15) — headcount planning failure; Compensation mismatch (15) — salary band not communicated early; Communication issues (12) — interviewer training gap; No show (9) — scheduling/engagement failure. Total controllable: 51 rejections (44.7% of all rejections). Candidate-driven rejections: Underqualified (13), Technical skills (11), Overqualified (10), Culture fit (10), Better candidate selected (8). Total: 52 (45.6%). Recommendation: half of all rejections are internally fixable.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"controllable_vs_uncontrollable\"},\"id\":\"rej-003\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Industry benchmarks for rejection patterns in technical hiring. Typical rejection rates: Engineering roles 55-65%, Data Science 50-60%, Product 45-55%. Expected early-stage (R1) rejection rate: 35-45% — screens out unqualified applicants. Expected compensation mismatch rate: 5-10% — above 12% signals JD salary range not being shared. Background check failure rate benchmark: 2-4%; above 8% suggests insufficient pre-screening. Culture fit rejection rate: 5-10%; above 12% may signal inconsistent culture definition across panels. No-show benchmark: under 5%; above 7% suggests poor candidate engagement or scheduling friction.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"industry_rejection_benchmarks\"},\"id\":\"rej-004\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Offer decline reason breakdown (23 declines). Team culture concerns: 6 (26.1%) — largest category, suggests culture evaluation gap. Competing offer: 3 (13.0%). Timeline too long: 3 (13.0%). Role scope mismatch: 3 (13.0%). Counter-offer accepted: 3 (13.0%). Compensation too low: 2 (8.7%). Personal reasons: 2 (8.7%). Relocation required: 1 (4.3%). Team culture concerns at 26% is unusually high — benchmark is 8-12%. Combined timeline + competing offer + counter-offer = 39% of declines are speed-related.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"offer_insights\",\"category\":\"decline_reasons\"},\"id\":\"offer-002\"}"}]

evaluation.reasoning: The insight correctly identifies process failures as a major rejection cause, aligning well with the golden dataset expectations.

insights.recommendation: Rebalance 15 panels from Sam Rivera to Casey Kim and Morgan Chen to reduce overload.

evidence.key_insight: Sam Rivera is 54-93% above the safe maximum load, risking burnout and scoring inconsistency.

alternative.trade_off: Higher cost and time for manual scheduling vs. quicker automated rebalancing.

insights.rag_context: [{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Interviewer panel load distribution (10 interviewers, 200 candidates). Sam Rivera: 77 panels (highest load, 38.5% of candidates). Jamie Osei: 64 panels. Riley Patel: 61. Alex Wang: 61. Drew Singh: 61. Taylor Brooks: 59. Quinn Diaz: 57. Jordan Lee: 56. Morgan Chen: 55. Casey Kim: 55. Average load: 60.6 panels per interviewer. Standard deviation: ~6.5. Sam Rivera is a significant outlier at 27% above average — risk of burnout and inconsistent scoring.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"panel_load_balancer\",\"category\":\"interviewer_load_summary\"},\"id\":\"panel-001\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Interviewer overload risk assessment. Industry benchmark: healthy interviewer load is 4-6 interviews per week, or 15-25 per month. Over a hiring cycle, 40-50 total panels is considered sustainable without calibration fatigue. Sam Rivera at 77 panels is 54-93% above safe maximum — high risk of scoring inconsistency. Jamie Osei at 64 is at the upper threshold. Casey Kim and Morgan Chen at 55 are within healthy range. Recommendation: cap any single interviewer at 65 panels per cycle; rebalance Sam Rivera immediately.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"panel_load_balancer\",\"category\":\"overload_risk\"},\"id\":\"panel-002\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Interview scoring consistency and panel quality benchmarks. Average cumulative round score across all 200 candidates: calculated from data. Score variance increases with interviewer fatigue — overloaded interviewers show wider score ranges. Industry standard: inter-rater reliability (IRR) should be above 0.7 (Cohen's kappa) for technical roles. Best practice: run calibration sessions every 30 panels per interviewer. Panels with 3+ interviewers have better IRR than 1-2 person panels. Recommendation: require minimum 2 interviewers per panel for all senior and above roles.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"panel_load_balancer\",\"category\":\"scoring_consistency\"},\"id\":\"panel-005\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Panel rebalancing strategy for overloaded interviewers. To rebalance: move 12-15 panels from Sam Rivera to Casey Kim and Morgan Chen (capacity available). Rotate Quinn Diaz (57) and Jordan Lee (56) into senior-level panels they haven't covered yet. Establish a panel rotation policy: no interviewer should be on more than 3 panels per week. Add a scheduling rule in ATS: flag any interviewer exceeding 70% of average panel load. Cross-train interviewers across role types to improve rebalancing flexibility.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"panel_load_balancer\",\"category\":\"rebalancing_strategy\"},\"id\":\"panel-003\"}"}]

evaluation.reasoning: The recommendation to rebalance panels is well-supported and matches the golden scenario for severe overload.

insights.recommendation: Reduce time-to-offer to under 5 days to decrease decline rate caused by long waiting times.

evidence.key_insight: Current decline rate is significantly higher than the industry benchmark of 20-30%, partly due to a long time-to-offer which affects 13% of declines.

alternative.trade_off: Lower cost but may provide less contextual accuracy regarding industry-specific benchmarks.

insights.rag_context: [{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Offer outcomes summary (57 offers made, 200 candidates total). Offers accepted: 34 (59.6% acceptance rate). Offers declined: 23 (40.4% decline rate). Overall pipeline conversion: 34 hires from 200 candidates = 17% end-to-end conversion. Industry benchmark for offer acceptance: 70-80% for senior engineering roles. Current 59.6% acceptance rate is 10-20 points below benchmark — significant gap requiring action. Each declined offer represents ~60 hours of interview time wasted across panels.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"offer_insights\",\"category\":\"offer_acceptance_summary\"},\"id\":\"offer-001\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Actions to improve offer acceptance rate from 59.6% to benchmark 75%+. (1) Address culture concerns: add team lunch/coffee with potential teammates before offer stage. (2) Reduce time-to-offer: 13% declined due to long timeline — target under 5 days from final interview to offer. (3) Competing offers: implement exploding offer policy with 72-hour decision window for senior roles. (4) Counter-offers: brief candidates on counter-offer likelihood during offer call. (5) Role scope: align JD with actual role scope — 3 declines suggest role was mis-scoped. (6) Compensation: ensure offer is within 5% of candidate's stated expectation gathered at screening.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"offer_insights\",\"category\":\"offer_improvement_actions\"},\"id\":\"offer-003\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Offer acceptance rates by sourcing channel. Company Website converts best once an offer is made: 85.7% acceptance rate (6 of 7 offers). Glassdoor is second at 77.8% (7 of 9). AngelList and LinkedIn both sit at 50.0%. Hackathon is the weakest closer at 40.0% — candidates from events may be passively exploring. Benchmark: industry average offer acceptance is 70-75%; channels below 55% warrant re-evaluation.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"sourcing_quality\",\"category\":\"channel_acceptance\"},\"id\":\"src-002\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Industry benchmarks for rejection patterns in technical hiring. Typical rejection rates: Engineering roles 55-65%, Data Science 50-60%, Product 45-55%. Expected early-stage (R1) rejection rate: 35-45% — screens out unqualified applicants. Expected compensation mismatch rate: 5-10% — above 12% signals JD salary range not being shared. Background check failure rate benchmark: 2-4%; above 8% suggests insufficient pre-screening. Culture fit rejection rate: 5-10%; above 12% may signal inconsistent culture definition across panels. No-show benchmark: under 5%; above 7% suggests poor candidate engagement or scheduling friction.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"industry_rejection_benchmarks\"},\"id\":\"rej-004\"}"}]

evaluation.reasoning: The insight correctly identifies the need to reduce time-to-offer, aligning well with the golden scenario for low acceptance rates.

insights.recommendation: Prioritize addressing SLA breaches by streamlining the hiring process for Senior and Lead Engineering roles to reduce time-to-hire.

evidence.key_insight: Current average days in pipeline exceeds SLA targets for multiple Senior and Lead roles, indicating a need for process optimization.

alternative.trade_off: Higher cost for more detailed insights.

insights.rag_context: [{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Overall pipeline funnel summary (200 candidates). Applications: 200. Reached offer stage: 57 (28.5%). Accepted offers: 34 (17.0%). Rejected: 114 (57.0%). Still active/not yet decided: 29 (14.5%). Position type breakdown: Engineering 102 (51%), Data Science 41 (20.5%), Product 38 (19%), Design 19 (9.5%). Position level: Senior 81 (40.5%), Staff 56 (28%), Lead 25 (12.5%), Mid 25 (12.5%), Principal 13 (6.5%). Pipeline is heavily skewed toward Senior and Staff levels — calibrate SLA targets accordingly.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"pipeline_health\",\"category\":\"pipeline_funnel_summary\"},\"id\":\"pipe-001\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Time-to-hire SLA benchmarks by position level. Mid-level roles: SLA target 21-28 days from application to offer. Senior roles: SLA target 28-35 days. Lead roles: SLA target 35-42 days. Staff roles: SLA target 35-45 days. Principal roles: SLA target 42-55 days. Average rounds by level in this dataset: Lead 3.32, Senior 3.01, Mid 2.84, Principal 2.54, Staff 2.39. Staff has fewer rounds but longer calendar time due to scheduling complexity. Every reschedule adds 3-5 days; 50% of candidates had 2+ reschedules — expect pipeline to run long.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"pipeline_health\",\"category\":\"sla_benchmarks_by_level\"},\"id\":\"pipe-002\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Sourcing effectiveness by position type. Engineering roles (102 of 200 candidates, 51%) are sourced across all channels. Data Science (41 candidates, 20.5%) responds best to Hackathon and Referral. Product (38 candidates, 19%) sourced primarily via Company Website and LinkedIn. Design (19 candidates, 9.5%) — smallest pool — sourced mostly via Referral and Glassdoor. Recommendation: tailor channel strategy per role type rather than using a single mix.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"sourcing_quality\",\"category\":\"role_type_sourcing\"},\"id\":\"src-005\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Pipeline velocity benchmarks for technical hiring. Industry average time-to-hire by role: Software Engineer 35-45 days, Senior Engineer 42-52 days, Staff/Principal 55-70 days, Data Scientist 40-55 days. Interview-to-offer conversion benchmark: 35-45% (current dataset: 28.5% — below benchmark). Offer-to-acceptance conversion benchmark: 72-78% (current: 59.6% — below benchmark). Sourcing-to-screen conversion: typically 20-30% of applicants reach phone screen. Pipeline health score formula: (Offer rate × Acceptance rate) / SLA compliance × 100.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"pipeline_health\",\"category\":\"velocity_metrics\"},\"id\":\"pipe-004\"}"}]

evaluation.reasoning: The recommendation to address SLA breaches is well-grounded and aligns perfectly with the golden scenario for multiple senior role breaches.
